task_completion.description: Configure remote syslog listener in ossec.conf
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal or text editor showing the 'ossec.conf' file. It must contain a '<remote>' block with '<connection>syslog</connection>', '<port>514</port>', '<protocol>udp</protocol>', and '<allowed-ips>172.16.0.0/12</allowed-ips>'.

task_completion.description: Create custom detection rule with correct ID and level
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal or text editor showing the 'local_rules.xml' file. It must contain a rule definition starting with '<rule id="100100" level="5">' (the level can be 5 or any number higher than 5).

task_completion.description: Configure the detection rule to match the specific Cisco ASA string
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the rule 100100 block in 'local_rules.xml', look for a match condition containing the exact string: '<match>%ASA-4-106023</match>'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the Wazuh manager configuration is active
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing a successful restart of the Wazuh manager service, such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart' completing without errors.

task_completion.description: Verify the syslog port is listening
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output from a network command like 'ss -uln', 'netstat -uln', or 'lsof -i:514' showing that UDP port 514 is open and listening.

integrity.description: Verify that ossec.conf and local_rules.xml were actually edited in their proper system paths
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the file paths shown in the editor or terminal commands correspond to the actual Wazuh installation directories (typically /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf and /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml), not temporary or mock files.

integrity.description: Verify that the service restart and port checks are genuine terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual shell prompts executing commands like 'systemctl' or 'ss'/'netstat' with realistic system output, rather than simply echoing text to the screen.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just create a fake log file with the expected output
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent configured the Wazuh manager to ingest the logs dynamically rather than manually writing the test log string directly into Wazuh's internal alert logs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target port: 514. Target protocol: UDP. Allowed IPs must include '172.16.0.0/12' (or '172.16'). Rule ID must be exactly 100100. Rule match string must be exactly '%ASA-4-106023'. If the agent tests the rule, the exact test log is: '<134>Mar 15 10:00:00 cisco-asa %ASA-4-106023: Deny tcp src outside:198.51.100.1/443 dst inside:10.0.0.5/80'.
